text:
Snoop Doggis a rapper, producer, actor and to add to his evergrowing list, he is now asports commentatorfor theParisOlympics2024.

His latest gig may come as a surprise to some people, but Snoop, real name Calvin Cordozar Broadus Jr, has never been a guy who can be put in a box.

As well as producing hits that can fill dancefloors quicker than you can say Drop It Like It’s Hot, he’s been switching up his occupation more often thanBarbie.

Follow Metro.co.uk’s live blog for latest updates on the 2024 Olympic Games

There is pet product company Snoop Doggie Doggs, cooking show Potluck Dinner Party with bestieMartha Stewart, and co-founding Casa Verde Capital –  a venture capital firm focused on investing in the cannabis industry, to name just a few of his side hustles.

While we know the 52-year-old has a passion for exploring varied avenues, how did he convince NBC and Peacock he was the perfect choice to be a special correspondent at the upcoming games?

Well, he showed his credentials in 2021, during theTokyoOlympics. Snoop provided memorable commentary on Olympic Highlights WithKevin Hartand Snoop Dogg, and was responsible for one particularly memorable moment.

In a now-viral moment, the pals watched an equestrian dressage competition andgave their instant reactions.

‘Oh the horse quip walking, you see that? On the set! That’s gangster,’ Snoop remarked at a clip of a horse performing the half-pass.

The Crip Walk, also known as the C-Walk, is a popular dance move that originated in Compton,California, in the 1970s after being created by first-generation Crip gang member Robert ‘Sugar Bear’ Jackson.

This dance involves the movement of one’s feet, classically to the spelling of C-R-I-P, and has become well-known within the Hip hop industry.

‘This horse is off the chain, I got to get this motherf****r in a video,’ he added.

The hilarious off-the-cuff comment led him on the path to his 2024 position, and to seemingly get the chance to meet the animal featured in the hilarious clip.

‘I’m going to meet the horse in real life. He’s the returning champ,’ he revealed earlier this year.

‘So I got to go meet him — you know what I’m saying? — and let him know I’m a big fan.’

Snoop planned to give the four-legged legend carrots, or some apples, and ask: ‘What’s Happenin’?’

However, there could be some bad news – the horse he made the comments about was Bella Rose 2, who was ridden by Isabell Werth forGermany. They won gold in the team event, but the horse retired after their win. Let’s hope there are some more crip-walking horses for Snoop to meet.

Snoop previously explained why he decided to stay yes in the first place to the job, which has led him to making the capital of France his home until August 11.

‘Why not? Not why. This is what I do, you know what I’m saying?’ he toldTime.

‘I love being in front of the people. I love sports. I know what I’m talking about. The network is appealing enough to understand that we deserve each other. So we’re gonna make magic.’

He also took the chance to state his intention to not smoke marijuana while inFranceas it’s illegal.

‘I’m a very legal guy,’ he said. ‘I plan on going out there and doing NBC Olympic work. Being clean as a book, clean as the athletes. They can test me if they want to.

‘I’m going to be out there doing what I’m supposed to be doing to make sure I bring home the gold. Which is me.’

His job will entail visiting historic landmarks, attending events, and speaking with athletes as well as their families. Snoop is most keen to ‘roll with the [U.S.] basketball team’ and get in a swimming pool so he can try to do the 50-metre freestyle in 50 seconds.

Snoop,who carried the Olympic torch before the opening ceremony, originally announced his participation on January 1st in a press release and said: ‘I grew up watching the Olympics and am thrilled to see the incredible athletes bring their A-game to Paris.

‘It’s a celebration of skill, dedication, and the pursuit of greatness,’ said Snoop. ‘We’re going to have some amazing competitions and, of course, I will be bringing that Snoop style to the mix.

The oral‑care gadget making waves thanks to its powerful clean

‘It’s going to be the most epic Olympics ever, so stay tuned, and keep it locked. Let’s elevate, celebrate, and make these games unforgettable, smoke the competition, and may the best shine like gold. Peace and Olympic LOVE, ya dig?’

title: Why is Snoop Dogg a commentator for Paris Olympics 2024? | Metro News
description: Snoop Dogg is a rapper, producer, actor and now a sports commentator for the Paris Olympics 2024, but how did he land the prestigious job?
